design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate flip-screen arenas, eliminate pirate enemies and hazards, collect power-ups and stolen loot, advance through progressively complex screen layouts within time constraints
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (ammunition, health) versus enemy density and environmental hazards; time pressure to retrieve stolen goods
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting an armed Federation combat vessel on a retrieval/revenge mission against space pirates, upgrading weapons and defenses through collected power-ups
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes tactical screen-by-screen navigation over continuous scrolling; requires pattern analysis and deliberate movement rather than reflexive dodging. Power-up permanence and variety (rear-gun, spiked ball orbiter, shields, homing missiles) create meaningful strategic loadout decisions

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives with loss condition; player must learn enemy placements and hazard patterns per screen
difficulty.checkpointing: Flip-screen checkpointing within levels; failure on a screen requires restart from that screen or level beginning
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Power-up collection provides tactical flexibility in approaching screens; choice of which weapons to prioritize affects difficulty perception, but core challenge is pre-determined screen layouts

progression.upgrade_system: Drop-based power-ups collected from defeated pirate ships: rear-gun, spiked ball orbiter, shields, homing missiles. Power-ups provide temporary or permanent weapon enhancements
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level progression locked behind completion of all screens in current level; time limit gate on mission completion
replayability_hooks: Optimization of screen-clear strategies to improve speed-running potential
Experimentation with different power-up loadout orders
High-score chasing through efficient enemy elimination

world_building_techniques: Mission briefing (pirates raided Federation storage, Cybernoid dispatched to retrieve stolen minerals/jewels/ammunition/weaponry)
Time-pressure narrative device (must return stolen booty within specified time limit)
Title screen in-jokes (NES version: ship designed by 'M. Sugden', humorous reference to British TV actress)

faction_indicators: Federation (player faction) - represented by Cybernoid ship pursuing retrieval mission
Space Pirates - antagonist faction plundering Federation storage depots
Planetary Defense Systems - environmental faction aligned with pirates
danger_signals: Homing missiles indicating hostile automated defenses
Gun turrets on fixed paths signaling fortified positions
Indestructible enemies on fixed paths creating environmental hazards requiring navigation skill
environmental_storytelling: Screen layouts reflect pirate stronghold architecture (varied hazard mixes suggest different facility types: weapons depot, command center, escape route)
Collision with enemies or environmental hazards visualizes Federation incursion against prepared defenses
